steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “proved this idea wrong by ______” is the decisive clue. The blank must name an action that would establish something as fact—in other words, an action that proves.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Demonstrating” means showing or proving something clearly. This is exactly how the researchers proved the old belief wrong—by showing evidence of three distinct species. The other choices don't express establishing a fact: “pretending” contradicts the idea of proving, “complaining” has no connection to proof, and “requiring” means demanding rather than showing. That matches choice D — D is correct.
